description: Definitions for the three importance levels used to classify how essential a skill is for a given role. Maps to the frontend ImportanceLevel type (critical | recommended | nice-to-have).

levels.label: Critical
levels.description: Must-have skill. The role cannot be performed without it. Employers will filter candidates on this. Should appear in every relevant job description.

levels.label: Recommended
levels.description: Strongly preferred skill. Frequently listed in job descriptions. Having this skill significantly improves candidacy, but some roles may accept candidates willing to learn it on the job.

levels.label: Nice to Have
levels.description: Differentiator skill. Not required, but sets candidates apart. Often listed under 'bonus' or 'preferred' in job descriptions. Good for career growth but not a barrier to entry.
